summary: McKenzie compares Yahoo and Google's advertising platforms after signing up for Yahoo's promotional credit. He systematically demonstrates why Google dominates online advertising through superior keyword tools, cleaner URL display, easier ad creation and testing, faster approval times, and the inclusion of Google Analytics. The post uses his direct experience as a small software business owner to illustrate the practical differences between the two platforms. His conclusion is implicit but clear: Google's advertising infrastructure is vastly more sophisticated and user-friendly than Yahoo's.
key_points: Google's keyword suggestion tools are far superior, finding semantically related terms that yield cheaper, less competitive advertising opportunities
Google enables A/B testing of ad variations with automatic optimization, while Yahoo requires repeating the entire setup process
Google Analytics bundled with AdWords provides conversion tracking and ROI measurement that Yahoo completely lacks
Small details like URL display and approval speed compound into a vastly better advertiser experience on Google
money_quotes: It was pretty immediately apparent why Google is kicking Yahoo's hindquarters.
Those words are cheaper than anything as nobody is targetting obscure teaching terminology… except me.
Google Analytics is worth keeping an AdWords account running for even if nobody ever converts from it, because it beats whatever web stats software you are currently using hands down.
Google will have you up in minutes. Yahoo will have you up and running in 72 hours.
If you've already got one ad in a keyword group, throw in another and Google will rotate them for you trying to find the one that gets clicked on most. Yahoo, not so much.
key_insight: Google's advertising dominance stems not from any single feature but from compounding advantages in tooling, speed, analytics, and advertiser experience that make Yahoo's platform feel like a generation behind.
